steps.title: Determine the yearly growth factor
steps.content:
A increase of 170% means the quantity becomes $100\% + 170\% = 270\%$ of the previous amount. In decimal form, that is $2.70$.
Thus each year the population is multiplied by $2.7$.
The model therefore has the form $$n = n_0 \cdot (2.7)^t$$ where $n_0$ is the population at $t=0$ (end of 2015).

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice B
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (n=80(2.7)^t):
Substitute $t=1$: $$n = 80 \cdot (2.7)^1 = 80 \cdot 2.7 = 216.$$
This equals the given population at the end of 2016, so choice B satisfies the condition.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
